coaching.coaching: State the concrete attestation evidence the [operator-attested] actual public constant names in gate_dispatch.py will require (a change id / vote outcome / timestamp), recorded on the ticket.
coaching.subject: actual public constant names in gate_dispatch.py

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

evidence: No `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` items found.
findings.finding: The ticket has no `## Acceptance Criteria` checklist. A plan cannot be reviewed for completion without testable, checkable criteria.
findings.suggested_fix: Add an `## Acceptance Criteria` section with `- [ ]` checklist items, one per observable, in-session-verifiable outcome.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's step-id literal names to match the actual public names in gate_dispatch.py: change `_STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE` to `STEP_VERIFY/STEP_DECIDE` (no underscore prefix) in the description of finalize.py's local re-declarations.
evidence: gate_dispatch.py line 56: `STEP_VERIFY = 'verify'` (public, no underscore)
gate_dispatch.py line 57: `STEP_DECIDE = 'decide'` (public, no underscore)
gate_dispatch.py line 217: `_LLM_STEP_KINDS = frozenset({...})` (private, underscore — correctly named in plan)
gate_dispatch.py lines 795/797: moved body references `STEP_VERIFY` and `STEP_DECIDE` by their public names
test_plan_review_verify_recovery.py:286 calls `gate_dispatch.STEP_VERIFY` — public name used externally
Plan states 'no signature or logic changes' — implementer cannot rename the references inside the moved body
findings.finding: The plan specifies that finalize.py re-declares step-id literals named `_STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE` (underscore-prefixed), but the actual constant names in gate_dispatch.py are `STEP_VERIFY` (line 56) and `STEP_DECIDE` (line 57) — public names with no underscore. The body of `_attach_code_review_metrics` being moved references `STEP_VERIFY` (line 795) and `STEP_DECIDE` (line 797); an implementer following the plan's naming spec literally would declare `_STEP_VERIFY = 'verify'` and `_STEP_DECIDE = 'decide'` as the local copies, causing an immediate NameError in the moved function unless the references are also renamed — which the plan prohibits ('no signature or logic changes').
findings.location: Plan body: 'The leaf is a STRICT LEAF: it re-declares the plan-review-shared step-id literals (_STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE/_LLM_STEP_KINDS)'
scenarios: Implementer creates `_STEP_VERIFY = 'verify'` in finalize.py (matching plan's underscore naming), moves _attach_code_review_metrics whose body references `STEP_VERIFY` (no underscore) → NameError at runtime when _attach_code_review_metrics runs.
Implementer silently 'fixes' the mismatch by renaming references inside the body — violating the plan's 'no logic changes' constraint and creating an undocumented deviation.
findings.suggested_fix: Replace '_STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE/_LLM_STEP_KINDS' with 'STEP_VERIFY/STEP_DECIDE/_LLM_STEP_KINDS' in the plan's STRICT LEAF description. The local re-declarations in finalize.py should be `STEP_VERIFY = "verify"`, `STEP_DECIDE = "decide"`, `_LLM_STEP_KINDS = frozenset({"agent", "batch"})` — matching the actual names used by the moved function body.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the plan's step-id literal names from `_STEP_VERIFY`/`_STEP_DECIDE` (underscore-prefixed) to `STEP_VERIFY`/`STEP_DECIDE` (their actual public names in gate_dispatch.py lines 56–57), so the implementer copies the right identifiers into finalize.py's local re-declarations.
evidence: gate_dispatch.py line 56: 'STEP_VERIFY = "verify"' (public, no underscore)
gate_dispatch.py line 57: 'STEP_DECIDE = "decide"' (public, no underscore)
_attach_code_review_metrics at lines 795/797 uses 'STEP_VERIFY' and 'STEP_DECIDE' (no underscore)
_LLM_STEP_KINDS at line 217 correctly has an underscore — only STEP_VERIFY/STEP_DECIDE are misnamed in the plan
findings.finding: The plan mislabels the step-id constants that finalize.py must re-declare as '_STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE' (underscore-prefixed), but the actual names in gate_dispatch.py are 'STEP_VERIFY' and 'STEP_DECIDE' (public, no leading underscore, lines 56–57). An implementer following the plan would search for non-existent '_STEP_VERIFY'/'_STEP_DECIDE' names to copy into the leaf, potentially introducing a typo or naming divergence.
findings.location: Plan body: 'The leaf is a STRICT LEAF: it re-declares the plan-review-shared step-id literals (_STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE/_LLM_STEP_KINDS) as small local constants'
scenarios: Implementer creates local constants '_STEP_VERIFY = "verify"' and '_STEP_DECIDE = "decide"' in finalize.py instead of 'STEP_VERIFY' / 'STEP_DECIDE', introducing a naming divergence with the rest of the codebase and a dead-letter mirror comment.
A future rename-check that validates the leaf's constant names against gate_dispatch.py's exported names would fail silently.
findings.suggested_fix: Change '(_STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE/_LLM_STEP_KINDS)' to '(STEP_VERIFY/STEP_DECIDE/_LLM_STEP_KINDS)' in the strict-leaf description, noting that the leaf's local copies should use the same public names (or rename them private with a mirror comment that shows the original name they shadow).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify in the plan that STEP_VERIFY and STEP_DECIDE are public (no underscore) in gate_dispatch.py — the finalize.py local re-declarations should be named _STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE (private) and the mirror comment should explicitly reference their public counterparts (STEP_VERIFY, STEP_DECIDE) to avoid confusion.
evidence: gate_dispatch.py line 56: STEP_VERIFY = "verify"
gate_dispatch.py line 57: STEP_DECIDE = "decide"
gate_dispatch.py line 217: _LLM_STEP_KINDS = frozenset({"agent", "batch"}) — this one IS private (underscore) and matches the plan
Plan text uses '_STEP_VERIFY' and '_STEP_DECIDE' with underscore prefix, mismatching the public names
findings.finding: The plan refers to the re-declared local constants in finalize.py as '_STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE' (private, underscore-prefixed), but the actual source symbols in gate_dispatch.py are STEP_VERIFY and STEP_DECIDE (public, no leading underscore). An implementer following the plan literally would name the finalize.py locals '_STEP_VERIFY'/'_STEP_DECIDE' while the mirror comment would need to say they shadow 'STEP_VERIFY'/'STEP_DECIDE' — a confusion hazard. The plan's naming should match reality.
findings.location: Plan body: 'The leaf is a STRICT LEAF: it re-declares the plan-review-shared step-id literals (_STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE/_LLM_STEP_KINDS) as small local constants with a mirror comment'
scenarios: Implementer creates finalize.py with '_STEP_VERIFY = "verify"' but the mirror comment says it shadows 'STEP_VERIFY' — harmless but confusing
A future reader of finalize.py cannot easily grep-verify the mirror claim because the names differ
findings.suggested_fix: Update plan text to: 're-declares the plan-review-shared step-id literals (STEP_VERIFY → local _STEP_VERIFY, STEP_DECIDE → local _STEP_DECIDE, _LLM_STEP_KINDS → local _LLM_STEP_KINDS) as small private local constants with a mirror comment naming the gate_dispatch originals'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether `finalize.py` can import `_LLM_STEP_KINDS` from `runs.py` (not `gate_dispatch`) to avoid a third independent copy — `runs.py` is upstream of `gate_dispatch.py` so no back-import cycle is introduced; if a true strict-leaf policy requires no imports from the `workflow/` package at all, document that constraint explicitly so the mirror-comment rationale is unambiguous.
evidence: gate_dispatch.py:217: `_LLM_STEP_KINDS = frozenset({"agent", "batch"})  # the billable LLM tier`
runs.py:306: `_LLM_STEP_KINDS = frozenset({"agent", "batch"})  # the billable, file-reading tool tiers`
gate_dispatch.py already imports from runs.py (e.g. `from .runs import RunnerAgentStep`), so runs.py is upstream of gate_dispatch.py — finalize.py importing from runs.py would not create a cycle.
Plan states the rationale as 'avoiding a back-import', but back-import applies to gate_dispatch (which finalize.py must not import), not to runs.py.
findings.finding: The plan proposes adding a third local copy of `_LLM_STEP_KINDS = frozenset({'agent', 'batch'})` in `finalize.py`, but the justification ('avoiding a back-import') does not hold for `runs.py`, which already defines the same constant and is not in finalize.py's back-import path (gate_dispatch imports FROM runs, not the reverse — so finalize.py importing from runs.py would be a forward dependency, not circular).
findings.location: Plan body: 'The leaf is a STRICT LEAF: it re-declares the plan-review-shared step-id literals (_STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE/_LLM_STEP_KINDS) as small local constants with a mirror comment, avoiding a back-import.'
scenarios: Three independent copies of the same two-element frozenset diverge silently if a new step kind (e.g. 'map') is added as LLM-tier — two copies are updated and one is missed, causing finalize.py to misclassify step timings.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an AC (or extend an existing one) that verifies `finalize_code_review_verdict` is importable from `finalize.py` and that `finalize.py` contains no import of `gate_dispatch` (strict-leaf constraint), e.g. `grep -R 'from.*gate_dispatch' src/rebar/llm/code_review/finalize.py` returns empty.
evidence: 'factor the success-branch body of _run_code_review_gate into a new finalize_code_review_verdict(...) in the leaf'
're-declares the plan-review-shared step-id literals (_STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE/_LLM_STEP_KINDS) as small local constants with a mirror comment'
No AC line checks for the existence of finalize_code_review_verdict or the mirror constants — only LOC bounds and test passage are checked.
findings.finding: The plan describes two concrete implementation deliverables — the new `finalize_code_review_verdict(...)` function factored from the success-branch body, and the local re-declaration of `_STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE/_LLM_STEP_KINDS` as mirror constants — but no acceptance criterion directly verifies either of these exists or is correctly formed. The existing AC set covers LOC bounds, re-exports, config deletions, and test passage, but a passing test suite is only an indirect oracle for the correct factoring (e.g., if the success-branch body were left in gate_dispatch and the function stub in finalize simply delegated nothing, tests might still pass while the described deliverable is absent).
findings.location: Plan body ('factor the success-branch body of _run_code_review_gate into a new finalize_code_review_verdict(...)') vs. Acceptance Criteria list
scenarios: An implementer moves only a subset of the named items and still satisfies all ACs if tests happen to pass, leaving the plan's described decomposition unverified.
A reviewer cannot confirm the strict-leaf constraint (no back-import) from any AC — only a manual grep or import-graph check would catch it.
findings.suggested_fix: Add to Acceptance Criteria: 'finalize_code_review_verdict is importable from src/rebar/llm/code_review/finalize (python -c "from rebar.llm.code_review.finalize import finalize_code_review_verdict"); finalize.py contains no import of gate_dispatch (grep -R gate_dispatch src/rebar/llm/code_review/finalize.py is empty).'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an explicit rollback/back-out statement to the plan (e.g., 'If any listed test regresses post-merge, revert via `git revert <sha>`; the re-export shim ensures no external interface changes need unwinding').
evidence: 'This is a pure/mechanical relocation — no signature or logic changes'
Re-export shim is described for _attach_code_review_metrics but no revert procedure is stated anywhere in the plan.
T4 criterion: 'The remedy is an explicit rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract — acknowledgement alone is insufficient.'
findings.finding: The plan has no explicit rollback or back-out path. If finalize.py introduction causes an unanticipated failure (e.g., a circular import at runtime not caught by the listed test suite, or a deployment environment where the new file is not picked up), there is no stated procedure to revert. T4 requires the remedy to be an explicit rollback/back-out plan or expand-contract sequence, not just acknowledgement of the breakage.
findings.location: Plan body — no rollback/revert section present
scenarios: A lazy-import cycle is introduced that only manifests at runtime under a specific import order not covered by the listed unit tests; without a rollback plan the on-call engineer has no prescribed path.
The new finalize.py is not included in a packaging manifest and the deployed artifact silently falls back to an ImportError.
findings.suggested_fix: Append a one-line rollback note: 'Rollback: `git revert <merge-sha>` fully restores gate_dispatch.py; the re-export shim means no consumer code changes are needed to recover.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a note to AGENTS.md (or a comment block in architecture.md) documenting: (1) that finalize.py is a strict leaf whose literal redeclarations are intentional to avoid circular imports, and (2) that gate_dispatch's `_attach_code_review_metrics` re-export is load-bearing (back-compat for test_code_review_fp_ledger.py) and must not be removed.
evidence: 'The leaf is a STRICT LEAF: it re-declares the plan-review-shared step-id literals (_STEP_VERIFY/_STEP_DECIDE/_LLM_STEP_KINDS) as small local constants with a mirror comment, avoiding a back-import.'
'gate_dispatch re-exports _attach_code_review_metrics (# noqa: F401) — load-bearing: tests/unit/test_code_review_fp_ledger.py calls gate_dispatch._attach_code_review_metrics at 7 sites.'
ABSENCE: No AC line or plan section calls for adding a note to AGENTS.md or architecture.md explaining the strict-leaf contract for finalize.py or the load-bearing re-export.
The architecture.md offenders row is DELETED, not replaced with any note about finalize.py's architectural constraints.
findings.finding: The plan creates finalize.py as a 'strict leaf' with an unusual pattern (re-declaring step-id literals locally instead of importing them) and a load-bearing back-compat re-export in gate_dispatch.py, but does not call for updating AGENTS.md or architecture.md to capture these decisions durably. A future maintainer may remove the re-export noqa line (thinking it dead code) or 'fix' the literal duplication in finalize.py without knowing it is intentional architecture.
findings.location: Plan body — 'The leaf is a STRICT LEAF: it re-declares the plan-review-shared step-id literals… with a mirror comment' and 'gate_dispatch re-exports _attach_code_review_metrics (# noqa: F401) — load-bearing'
scenarios: A future developer sees duplicated step-id literals in finalize.py and 'deduplicates' them by importing from gate_dispatch, creating a circular import.
A linting or dead-code pass removes the # noqa: F401 re-export, silently breaking test_code_review_fp_ledger.py at 7 call sites.
findings.suggested_fix: In AGENTS.md (or a 'Module conventions' section of architecture.md), add two bullet points: one explaining the strict-leaf contract (no back-imports; literals redeclared locally with a 'mirror of gate_dispatch' comment), and one flagging the gate_dispatch re-export as a test-contract shim (cite the 7 call sites). Alternatively, add a bold # LOAD-BEARING RE-EXPORT comment directly above the noqa line in gate_dispatch.py, and a # STRICT LEAF — DO NOT IMPORT FROM gate_dispatch block at the top of finalize.py.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add the intended parameter list and return type for `finalize_code_review_verdict(...)` to the plan (e.g., `finalize_code_review_verdict(request: CodeReviewRequest, prep: _CodeReviewPrep, *, runner_sel, cfg, res, total_ms: float) -> dict[str, Any]`) so the new function's interface is unambiguously specified.
evidence: gate_dispatch.py lines 683–739: success-branch uses `runner_sel`, `cfg`, `prep` (_CodeReviewPrep), `request` (CodeReviewRequest), `res` from outer scope
Plan gives no parameter list for finalize_code_review_verdict
Plan gives no return type annotation for finalize_code_review_verdict
Plan gives no calling signature for the lazy-import call inside the residual _run_code_review_gate
ABSENCE: no AC verifies the function signature or its caller interface
findings.finding: The plan introduces `finalize_code_review_verdict(...)` as a new public function in finalize.py but never specifies its parameter list, return type, or calling convention. The success-branch body (gate_dispatch.py lines 683–739) uses multiple local variables from `_run_code_review_gate`'s scope: `runner_sel`, `cfg`, `prep` (a `_CodeReviewPrep` NamedTuple), `request` (a `CodeReviewRequest`), and `res`. Without a defined interface, the 'thin sequencer' description of the residual `_run_code_review_gate` is incomplete — an executor cannot know what to pass to `finalize_code_review_verdict` or verify the refactoring is correct via the test suite alone.
findings.location: Plan body: 'factor the success-branch body of _run_code_review_gate into a new finalize_code_review_verdict(...) in the leaf — leaving _run_code_review_gate a thin sequencer that lazily imports finalize'
scenarios: Executor passes (request, prep, runner_sel, cfg, res, total_ms) positionally — reasonable but unverified; a different executor passes a dict or restructures the arguments, producing an incompatible interface that breaks the session tests.
The lazy-import pattern changes the calling convention relative to what the test monkeypatches expect, silently breaking test_code_review_gate_session.py without the plan surfacing why.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
